entries.title: Chiller protection alarm E6
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic entry for a Gree chiller E6 alarm. Verify exact meaning and limits in the service manual for the installed chiller model.
likelyCauses: Protection input active because a measured operating condition is outside allowed range.
Water-flow, temperature-sensor, pressure, or electrical condition requiring model-specific confirmation.
Control board input or field wiring issue.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm model and operating mode
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record the chiller model, current operating mode, displayed E6 code, and any secondary controller diagnostics.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: The installed model and active diagnostic state are documented.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Do not classify the fault until the installed model and controller state are known.

diagnosticSteps.title: Check water-side operating condition
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should confirm water pump status, flow indication, strainer condition, and leaving/entering water temperatures.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Water-side flow and temperatures are within the expected service range for the operating mode.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Resolve water-side restrictions or pump faults using the approved service process.

diagnosticSteps.title: Inspect protection inputs
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Inspect relevant protection input wiring and sensors according to the exact chiller service manual.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Protection input wiring and sensors are intact and readings are plausible.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Repair wiring or sensor faults; replace components only after measurements support the conclusion.

requiredMeasurements.name: Entering and leaving water temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured water temperatures used to assess flow and operating condition.

requiredMeasurements.name: Water flow indication
requiredMeasurements.description: Flow switch, differential pressure, or flow-meter indication required by the installed chiller design.

requiredMeasurements.name: Supply voltage
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured supply voltage at the chiller service point by qualified personnel.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, inverter, refrigerant, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep safety switches, protection inputs, current protection, pressure protection, flow protection, and controller safeguards active during diagnosis.
De-energize and lock out equipment where the service procedure requires it, and follow the manufacturer manual and local safety rules.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.

entries.title: Chiller preliminary alarm E1
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree chiller E1 alarm. Verify exact meaning and limits in the service manual for the installed chiller model before drawing a final conclusion.
likelyCauses: Water-side, temperature, pressure, or electrical protection condition reported by the controller.
Pump, strainer, flow indication, or sensor input condition requiring qualified diagnosis.
Model-specific controller interpretation that must be verified against the exact chiller manual.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm chiller operating state
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record chiller model, displayed E1 code, operating mode, water pump state, and any secondary controller diagnostics.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Installed model and operating state are documented before diagnosis continues.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop classification until exact model and controller information are available.

diagnosticSteps.title: Check water-side condition
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should confirm pump operation, strainer condition, water flow indication, and entering/leaving water temperatures.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Water-side values are plausible for the active operating mode.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Resolve water-side restrictions or sensor issues using the approved service process.

requiredMeasurements.name: Entering and leaving water temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured water temperatures used to assess chiller operating condition.

requiredMeasurements.name: Water flow indication
requiredMeasurements.description: Flow switch, differential pressure, or flow-meter indication required by the installed chiller design.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, refrigerant, hydronic, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep manufacturer safeguards, flow protection, pressure protection, and controller protections active during diagnosis.
Verify the exact service manual before drawing a final diagnosis.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.

entries.title: Chiller preliminary alarm E2
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree chiller E2 alarm. Treat this as seed guidance until exact manual evidence is added.
likelyCauses: Temperature sensor, water-side condition, or controller input outside expected range.
Loose sensor wiring, unstable reading, or abnormal entering/leaving water condition.
Model-specific chiller protection logic requiring exact manual verification.

diagnosticSteps.title: Record temperature context
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record model, displayed E2 code, entering/leaving water temperatures, ambient condition, and pump status.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Temperature and operating context are documented before classification.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Collect complete chiller operating data before continuing.

diagnosticSteps.title: Compare sensor readings
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should compare controller temperature readings with measured water temperatures using the service procedure.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Controller readings are plausible and stable for the measured condition.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Follow the manufacturer diagnostic path for sensor, wiring, or water-side issues.

requiredMeasurements.name: Controller temperature reading
requiredMeasurements.description: Relevant controller-reported temperature value for the suspected alarm condition.

requiredMeasurements.name: Measured water temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured water temperature near the related sensor location by qualified personnel.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, refrigerant, hydronic, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep manufacturer safeguards, flow protection, pressure protection, and controller protections active during diagnosis.
Verify the exact service manual before drawing a final diagnosis.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.

entries.title: Chiller preliminary alarm E3
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree chiller E3 alarm. Verify exact meaning against the installed model service manual before final diagnosis.
likelyCauses: Pressure, water-flow, or heat-exchange condition reported by the chiller controller.
Restricted water circuit, abnormal pump operation, or heat exchanger fouling.
Pressure sensor, temperature sensor, or controller input condition requiring qualified diagnosis.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm water and refrigerant context
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record chiller model, displayed E3 code, entering/leaving water temperatures, flow indication, and ambient condition.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Operating conditions are documented before pressure or flow interpretation.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop classification until model and water-side data are available.

diagnosticSteps.title: Inspect flow and heat transfer
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should inspect pump operation, strainers, valves, heat exchanger condition, and required flow indication.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Water flow and heat transfer path are suitable for the active operating mode.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Correct water-side restrictions or sensor issues using the approved service process.

requiredMeasurements.name: Water flow indication
requiredMeasurements.description: Flow switch, differential pressure, or flow-meter indication required by the installed chiller design.

requiredMeasurements.name: Entering and leaving water temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured water temperatures used to interpret heat transfer.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, refrigerant, hydronic, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep manufacturer safeguards, flow protection, pressure protection, and controller protections active during diagnosis.
Verify the exact service manual before drawing a final diagnosis.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.

entries.title: Chiller preliminary alarm E4
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree chiller E4 alarm. Confirm the exact model-specific meaning and service limits before drawing a final conclusion.
likelyCauses: Compressor, refrigerant circuit, temperature, or pressure protection condition reported by the controller.
Abnormal heat rejection, water-side condition, or electrical operating value.
Sensor, wiring, or controller input issue requiring qualified service verification.

diagnosticSteps.title: Record chiller alarm history
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record model, displayed E4 code, operating mode, alarm recurrence, water temperatures, and any secondary controller diagnostics.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Alarm history and operating state are documented before component-level diagnosis.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Collect exact model and controller history before continuing.

diagnosticSteps.title: Check operating values
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should compare supply voltage, current, water temperatures, and controller diagnostic values with the exact service manual.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Measured values are within model-specific expectations for the active operating state.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Follow the manufacturer diagnostic path for the measured abnormal condition.

requiredMeasurements.name: Supply voltage
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured supply voltage at the chiller service point by qualified personnel.

requiredMeasurements.name: Operating current
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured compressor or pump current where the service procedure requires it.

requiredMeasurements.name: Controller diagnostic value
requiredMeasurements.description: Relevant controller value for the suspected E4 condition.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, refrigerant, hydronic, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep manufacturer safeguards, flow protection, pressure protection, and controller protections active during diagnosis.
Verify the exact service manual before drawing a final diagnosis.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.
